proven.claim: After a successful `fileIssue` call the function logs the returned URL
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 165
; After a successful `fileIssue` call the function logs the returned URL
; and increments `filed`.  The invariant: `filed` is exactly the number of
; successful filings performed so far.
(declare-const filed_before Int)
(declare-const filed_after Int)
(declare-const issueExists_ret Bool)
(declare-const fileIssue_succeeds Bool)

; pre‑condition: we are in the non‑dry‑run path and the issue is not a duplicate
(assert (= issueExists_ret false))
(assert (= fileIssue_succeeds true))

; transition: successful filing increments `filed`
(assert (= filed_after (+ filed_before 1)))

; negated property: `filed` does NOT increase after a successful filing
(assert (not (= filed_after (+ filed_before 1))))

(check-sat)

proven.claim: The log is printed only after a successful `fileIssue` call.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 165 (file‑success logging)
; The log is printed only after a successful `fileIssue` call.
; Prove that if the log appears then `filed` must have been increased.
(declare-const fileIssue_ret Bool)
(declare-const filed_before Int)
(declare-const filed_after Int)

; success of filing
(assert (= fileIssue_ret true))

; transition: successful filing increments `filed`
(assert (= filed_after (+ filed_before 1)))

; negated property: log appears while `filed` stays unchanged
(assert (and (= fileIssue_ret true) (not (= filed_after (+ filed_before 1)))))

(check-sat)
